mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and aggregate retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand growth, which sustains corporate earnings momentum and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in GDPC1 and RSAFS fail to predict positive 60-day returns (out-of-sample R-squared < 0.01 over 5-year walk-forward).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index signals manufacturing contraction that immediately pressures equity prices, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat following INDPRO declines in late-cycle regimes (hit rate < 40%).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month inflation reading that crosses above 0.35 percent triggers expectations of monetary policy tightening that compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate consumer spending capacity, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 60-day equity returns (Rank IC < 0.01 over 3-year rolling windows).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between housing starts and retail sales, where housing starts decline while retail sales stagnate, signals a broad demand collapse that immediately depresses S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The HOUST-RSAFS divergence signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns (out-of-sample accuracy < 50% over 4-year walk-forward).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in industrial production and fall in the unemployment rate confirms a synchronized supply-labor recovery that rapidly lifts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following a macroeconomic shock.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of triggered instances during post-shock recovery regimes.
